info.title: Cambium Fiber API v1 (Legacy)
info.description: **v1 API (Legacy)** - Function-based endpoints for backwards compatibility. Use [v2 API](/docs) for new integrations.

get.description:
Basic health check endpoint for monitoring and load balancers.

Returns simple JSON response indicating service is running.
**Authentication:** This endpoint does not require authentication.

Exposes operational metrics in Prometheus exposition format.

**Metrics included:**
- `http_request_duration_seconds` - Request latency histogram (method, endpoint, status_code)
- `http_requests_total` - Total HTTP requests counter (method, endpoint, status_code)
- `cache_hits_total` - Cache hit counter (cache_type)
- `cache_misses_total` - Cache miss counter (cache_type)
- `discovery_cache_serials` - Number of ONUs in discovery cache
- `discovery_cache_olts` - Number of OLTs in discovery cache
- `olt_healthy_total` - Number of healthy OLTs
- `olt_unreachable_total` - Number of unreachable OLTs

**Prometheus Configuration:**
```yaml
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'olt-api'
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['api-server:8000']
    metrics_path: '/metrics'
    scrape_interval: 30s
```

**Authentication:** This endpoint does not require authentication to allow
Prometheus scraper access without OAuth tokens.

text/plain.example:
# HELP http_requests_total Total HTTP requests
# TYPE http_requests_total counter
http_requests_total{method="GET",endpoint="/health",status_code="200"} 42.0

post.description:
Generate a comprehensive tech support diagnostic file.

Gathers system info, API configuration, OLT connectivity status,
and test results into a single downloadable JSON file for remote
tech support diagnosis.

Execute production validation tests against deployed API.

Tests run against pub/tests/ suite using real OAuth authentication.
All write operations use dry-run mode (safe for production).

The API base URL is automatically detected from the incoming request.
This allows the same endpoint to validate dev (port 8000) and
production (port 8192) environments.

Args:
    request: FastAPI request object (used to detect current host/port)
    validation_request: Validation request with optional category filtering
        and optional credential overrides (client_id, client_secret)

Returns:
    Test results including passed/failed/skipped counts and individual test details

Raises:
    HTTPException 429: If another test execution is already in progress
    HTTPException 500: If test execution fails or configuration invalid

Example:
    >>> # Run all tests with default credentials
    >>> POST /validate/run {"categories": ["health", "auth", "olts", "onus", "profiles", "services"]}
    >>> # Run with specific categories and credential override
    >>> POST /validate/run {
    ...     "categories": ["health", "auth"],
    ...     "client_id": "my-client",
    ...     "client_secret": "my-secret"
    ... }

Security:
    Requires HTTP Basic Auth (same as /docs endpoint)

500.description: Test execution failed or configuration error

get.description:
Get information about where OAuth credentials are currently being loaded from.

Returns:
    dict with:
    - source: "env_vars", "connections_json", or "none"
    - client_id: The currently active client ID (if available)
    - hint: User-friendly description of the credentials source

schema.title: Response Get Credentials Source Validate Creds Source Get

post.description:
Test if provided OAuth credentials are valid by attempting token acquisition.

Request body:
    - client_id: OAuth client ID to test
    - client_secret: OAuth client secret to test

Returns:
    dict with:
    - valid: bool indicating if credentials work
    - message: Human-readable result message
    - token_expires_in: Token expiration time (if valid)

schema.title: Response Test Credentials Validate Test Creds Post

get.description:
Return list of configured OLT IDs (v1 wrapped format for backward compatibility).

v2 will return array-only format.

schema.title: Response List Available Olt Ids Config System Olt Ids Get

get.description:
Get OLT device name (v1 endpoint).

Returns device name in wrapped format: ``{"device_name": "..."}``.  Requires
``olt_id`` query parameter.

Raises:
    404: OLT not found
    502: Bad gateway - OLT communication error

put.description:
Set OLT device name (v1 endpoint).

Request body: ``{"device_name": "new-name"}``.  Requires ``olt_id`` query
parameter.  Returns updated name in the same wrapped format.

Raises:
    400: Missing device_name field
    404: OLT not found
    502: Bad gateway - OLT communication error

get.description:
Retrieve application logs with pagination.

File extension determines format:
- /logs.json → JSON format (default)
- /logs.log or /logs.txt → Plain text format

Args:
    page: Page number (default: 1)
    per_page: Logs per page (default: 1000, max: 10000)
    level: Filter by log level (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL)

Returns:
    JSON: {"logs": [...], "pagination": {...}, "links": {...}}
    Text: Plain text formatted log lines

Examples:
    /config/system/logs.json?page=1&per_page=100
    /config/system/logs.log?level=ERROR&page=2

get.summary: View application logs as plain text

get.summary: [Deprecated] View logs with old format parameter
get.description:
[Deprecated] Use /logs.json or /logs.log instead.

Old endpoint maintained for backwards compatibility.
Converts limit to pagination (page 1 with limit as per_page).

Get the current application log level.

Returns:
    Current log level (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL) and available levels

put.description:
Set the application log level. Setting persists across restarts.

Args:
    payload: {"level": "DEBUG"|"INFO"|"WARNING"|"ERROR"|"CRITICAL"}

Returns:
    Updated log level

Examples:
    PUT /config/system/log_level
    {"level": "INFO"}

get.description:
Get current health tracking configuration.

Returns:
    Current health settings (connection_timeout, retry_interval, success_threshold)

Examples:
    GET /config/system/health_settings

put.description:
Update health tracking configuration.

Args:
    payload: Settings to update (connection_timeout, retry_interval, success_threshold)

Returns:
    Updated settings with confirmation message

Validation:
    - connection_timeout: 1-30 seconds
    - retry_interval: 10-300 seconds
    - success_threshold: 1-100 consecutive successes

Examples:
    PUT /config/system/health_settings
    {"connection_timeout": 3, "retry_interval": 60}

get.description:
Get current health status for all OLTs.

Returns detailed health information including:
- Current state (unknown/healthy/degraded)
- Success/failure counts
- Last attempt/failure timestamps
- Next retry time (for degraded OLTs)

Returns:
    Health status dict with OLT IDs as keys

Examples:
    GET /config/system/olt_health

post.description:
Backup OLT configuration to file using SSH.

Creates snapshot of complete OLT configuration (true backup, not HTTP API merge).
Uses admin credentials from connections.json.

System-level operations like backup/restore use direct OLTConnection
for SSH credentials but remain consistent with architectural patterns.

Args:
    olt_id: OLT identifier (e.g., 'F6ZF01C8P631')
    request: FastAPI request object for error handling

Returns:
    Backup metadata: {"backup_id": "20260127_143052", "path": "...", "size": 12345}

Raises:
    404: OLT not found
    500: SSH backup failed (connection, authentication, or command error)

Examples:
    POST /config/system/F6ZF01C8P631/backup

Restore OLT configuration from backup using SSH.

Performs true configuration restore (not HTTP API merge).
Uses admin credentials from connections.json.

System-level operations like backup/restore use direct OLTConnection
for SSH credentials but remain consistent with architectural patterns.

Args:
    olt_id: OLT identifier
    payload: {"backup_id": "20260127_143052"} or {"backup_file": "path/to/backup.json"}
    request: FastAPI request object for error handling

Returns:
    Restore result: {"status": "restored", "backup_id": "20260127_143052", "olt_id": "..."}

Raises:
    400: Missing backup_id or backup_file in payload
    404: OLT or backup not found
    500: SSH restore failed

Examples:
    POST /config/system/F6ZF01C8P631/restore
    {"backup_id": "20260127_143052"}

get.description:
List available configuration backups for OLT.

Args:
    olt_id: OLT identifier

Returns:
    List of backups: [{"backup_id": "...", "timestamp": "...", "size": 12345}, ...]

Examples:
    GET /config/system/F6ZF01C8P631/backups
